text: When does an election start to feel unfair to you?
Wann beginnt sich eine Wahl für dich unfair anzufühlen?
Should be easy or hard for new political parties to get into parliament?
Sollten neue Parteien leichter oder schwerer ins Parlament kommen?
In politics, should decisions be faster or more tightly checked?
Sollten Entscheidungen in der Politik schneller oder strenger geprüft werden?
Should everyone have an equal say, even if they are uninformed?
Sollte jeder das gleiche Mitspracherecht haben, auch wenn er nicht informiert ist?
Should elected officials represent all citizens?
Sollten gewählte Amtsträger alle Bürger vertreten?
Who do you trust more: local or national politicians?
Wem vertrauen Sie mehr: lokalen oder nationalen Politikern?
What would you change about how laws are enforced?
Was würden Sie an der Durchsetzung von Gesetzen ändern??
What kind of safety net would make society feel fairer to you?
Welche Art von Sicherheitsnetz würde die Gesellschaft für Sie gerechter machen?
What are the limits of free speech?
Wo liegen die Grenzen der Meinungsfreiheit?
How can we better protect minority rights?
Wie können wir Minderheitenrechte besser schützen?
What would make political participation easier for more people?
Was würde die politische Teilhabe für mehr Menschen erleichtern?
How independent from politics should courts be?
Wie unabhängig von der Politik sollten Gerichte sein?
What should happen if a popular leader breaks the law?
Was sollte passieren, wenn ein beliebter Politiker gegen das Gesetz verstößt?
How transparent should politicians be about decisions and finances?
Wie transparent sollten Politiker in Bezug auf Entscheidungen und Finanzen sein?
What makes a neighborhood feel safe?
Was macht ein Viertel sicher?
How big a role should activist groups or citizen movements play in shaping policy?
Wie groß sollte die Rolle von Aktivistengruppen oder Bürgerbewegungen bei der Gestaltung der Politik sein?
Which forms of participation actually make a difference?
Welche Formen der Beteiligung bewirken tatsächlich etwas?
Should voting be mandatory?
Sollte die Wahlpflicht eingeführt werden?
The state shall implement Proportional Representation to ensure legislative bodies accurately reflect the diverse ideological and demographic makeup of the citizenry.
A constitutionally independent Judiciary shall hold the absolute authority to nullify any executive action that infringes upon fundamental minority rights.
Public institutions must mandate inclusive deliberative assemblies where minority representatives possess a formal right of consultation over targeted policies.
Universal suffrage shall be protected as an inviolable right, with automatic voter registration for all citizens upon reaching the age of maturity.
The freedom of the press shall be absolute, prohibiting government censorship or the intimidation of journalists and whistleblowers.
Term limits shall be strictly enforced for all executive and legislative offices to prevent the consolidation of dynastic or permanent power.
All campaign financing must be transparent and publicly disclosed in real-time to prevent the undue influence of private capital on public policy.
The right to peaceful assembly and protest shall be guaranteed without the requirement of state-issued permits or prior restraint.
Civic education regarding democratic processes and human rights shall be a mandatory component of the national curriculum for all students.
Local municipalities shall be granted significant autonomy to manage regional affairs, ensuring governance remains close to the people.
All government data not pertaining to immediate national security shall be open-source and accessible to the public by default.
The drawing of electoral districts shall be performed by independent, non-partisan commissions to eliminate the practice of gerrymandering.
Whistleblower protection acts shall be established to shield employees who report corruption within both public and private sectors.
The right to a speedy and public trial by an impartial jury shall be maintained for all criminal and civil litigations.
No citizen shall be deprived of life, liberty, or property without due process of law and the right to legal counsel.
Political parties must adhere to democratic internal structures, including the open election of their leadership and candidates.
The military shall remain strictly under civilian control and is prohibited from intervening in domestic political disputes.
Access to high-speed internet shall be recognized as a fundamental utility to ensure equal participation in the digital democratic sphere.
Referendums on major constitutional changes shall be required to ensure direct popular consent for the foundations of governance.
The state shall provide equal protection under the law regardless of race, gender, religion, or socioeconomic status.
Public officials shall be subject to regular ethics audits and must divest from assets that present a conflict of interest.
The right to privacy against unreasonable searches and electronic surveillance shall be upheld as a cornerstone of individual liberty.
Labor unions and collective bargaining shall be protected to ensure the democratic voice of the worker in the national economy.
Electoral cycles shall be fixed and predictable, preventing sitting governments from manipulating election dates for political advantage.
Language rights for linguistic minorities shall be respected in all legal and administrative proceedings.
Environmental protection shall be a constitutional mandate to preserve the rights of future generations to a habitable planet.
The state shall maintain a strict separation of church and state to guarantee religious freedom for all and secularism in law.
Administrative decisions must be accompanied by written justifications and be subject to judicial review.
The right to petition the government for a redress of grievances shall be protected and must receive a formal response.
Lobbying activities must be strictly regulated and recorded in a public registry to maintain the integrity of the legislative process.
Public broadcasting shall be funded independently to provide unbiased information without government interference.
The state shall ensure equitable access to quality healthcare, viewing a healthy citizenry as essential for robust democratic participation.
Provisions for the impeachment or recall of elected officials shall be clearly defined for cases of gross misconduct.
Equal pay for equal work shall be enforced to ensure economic fairness and gender equality within the workforce.
The secrecy of the ballot shall be absolute, ensuring no citizen can be coerced or intimidated regarding their vote.
Indigenous populations shall have the right to self-determination and the protection of their ancestral lands.
The state shall provide legal aid to those who cannot afford representation, ensuring justice is not a privilege of the wealthy.
National census data shall be updated regularly to ensure accurate representation and fair allocation of public resources.
Political asylum shall be granted to those fleeing persecution, reflecting the democratic commitment to universal human rights.
The use of emergency powers by the executive shall be time-limited and require immediate legislative ratification.
The right to strike and engage in peaceful industrial action shall be recognized as a fundamental democratic liberty.
The state shall actively combat disinformation through the promotion of media literacy and the support of fact-checking initiatives.
Citizens' assemblies may be convened to provide recommendations on complex ethical or long-term policy issues.
All children shall have the right to a safe environment and protection from exploitation and abuse.
Public procurement and contracts shall be awarded through transparent, competitive bidding processes to prevent cronyism.
The state shall recognize the right to personal identity and the freedom of self-expression in all its forms.
Monopolies and trusts shall be regulated to ensure a competitive market that allows for economic social mobility.
Taxation shall be progressive, ensuring that the burden of public services is shared fairly based on the ability to pay.
The state shall facilitate the reintegration of formerly incarcerated individuals to ensure their return to full democratic participation.
The constitution shall be the supreme law of the land, and all public officials must swear an oath to uphold it above all else.
